class.name: HealthComponent
class.brief_description: An advanced component node that manages health logic, RPG stat-driven damage, and passive regeneration.
class.description: The [HealthComponent] is a modular [Node] written in C++ (GDExtension) designed to handle health tracking, healing, dynamic damage mitigation, and passive regeneration. It seamlessly integrates with [ActorStats] to automatically handle evasion and armor reduction. All changes to health trigger detailed event notifications, making it perfectly suited for turn-based and real-time RPG systems.

method.name: add_health
method.description: Adjusts [member current_health] by adding the specified [param p_health] value (supports negative values for raw damage). Automatically handles clamping, triggers the [signal health_changed] signal, checks entity vitality status, and returns the updated health value.

method.name: change_health
method.description: Allows manual, precise overriding of both [member max_health] and [member current_health] boundaries simultaneously. Commonly used for stat-driven percentage scaling or equipment changes.

method.name: get_health
method.description: Returns the current health points of the entity.

method.name: get_max_health
method.description: Returns the maximum health capacity of the entity.

method.name: get_regen_amount
method.description: Returns the current amount of health restored per regeneration tick.

method.name: get_regen_interval
method.description: Returns the current time interval between regeneration ticks.

method.name: is_alive
method.description: Checks whether [member current_health] is greater than zero. If the entity is dead and [param p_emit_signal] is [code]true[/code], it triggers the [signal died] signal exactly once.

method.name: set_health
method.description: Directly sets the [member current_health] to [param new_health], clamping it safely. Triggers the [signal health_changed] signal.

method.name: set_max_health
method.description: Sets the maximum health capacity ([member max_health]), keeping [member current_health] within safe boundaries.

method.name: set_regen_amount
method.description: Sets the amount of health restored per regeneration tick.

method.name: set_regen_interval
method.description: Sets the time interval between regeneration ticks.

method.name: set_regeneration
method.description: A convenience method to configure both regeneration parameters in a single call. Setting either parameter to [code]0.0[/code] safely deactivates the regeneration system.

method.name: take_damage
method.description: Processes incoming combat damage. Automatically queries the adjacent [ActorStats] component on the same entity to process random-rolled evasion checks and armor mitigation. Returns [code]true[/code] if the entity sustained damage, or [code]false[/code] if the attack was successfully evaded.

members.member: The current health points of the entity. Modifying this property automatically clamps the value between [code]0.0[/code] and [member max_health].
The maximum health capacity of the entity. Changing this value ensures [member current_health] never exceeds the new boundary.
The amount of health points recovered on each regeneration tick.
The time interval (in seconds) between each regeneration tick.

signal.name: died
signal.description: Emitted immediately when [member current_health] drops to [code]0.0[/code] and the entity is verified as dead.

signal.name: health_changed
signal.description: Emitted every time [member current_health] changes through healing, regeneration, or damage. Provides [param next_health] (the updated value) and [param prev_health] (the state before modification), which is highly practical for smooth UI synchronization.
